incidents.attacker: Iran — Ministry of Intelligence (MOIS)-linked hacktivist proxies
incidents.vector: Compromise of Microsoft endpoint management systems (Intune) used to push destructive wiper payloads across the enterprise.
incidents.impact: Data-wiping attack claimed to have erased 200,000+ systems across 79 countries; 5,000+ workers sent home in Ireland; offices globally shut down. CISA issued endpoint management hardening alert citing this incident.

incidents.vector: Cascading supply chain attack: TeamPCP compromised Aqua Security's Trivy vulnerability scanner via stolen credentials, then used that access to compromise Checkmarx KICS and the liteLLM AI proxy package on PyPI.
incidents.impact: Credential-stealing malware injected into official releases of three widely-used open-source security and AI tools, harvesting SSH keys, cloud tokens, API keys, and crypto wallets from downstream users. liteLLM has 3 million daily downloads.

incidents.vector: TeamPCP deployed CanisterWorm through exposed Docker APIs, Kubernetes clusters, and Redis servers, using blockchain-based ICP canisters for command and control.
incidents.impact: Wiper component targets systems with Iranian timezone or Farsi locale, destroying data on entire Kubernetes clusters. Extent of actual data destruction is unconfirmed.

incidents.vector: Exploitation of CVE-2026-33017 (CVSS 9.8), a code injection flaw in Langflow's public flow build endpoint that passes attacker-controlled Python code to exec() without sandboxing.
incidents.impact: Attackers extracted API keys for OpenAI, Anthropic, AWS, and other services from vulnerable instances within hours of disclosure. CISA added to KEV catalog.

incidents.vector: Ransomware deployed to servers managing cargo traffic and digital services at Spain's major fishing port.
incidents.impact: Digital cargo management systems taken offline; port forced to manual operations and paper documentation. No group has claimed responsibility. Investigation ongoing into potential data compromise.

incidents.vector: Ransomware attack shutting down all city IT systems except emergency services.
incidents.impact: All public services suspended for 30,000+ residents; officials considering declaring a state of emergency to receive state and federal assistance.

incidents.vector: Three distinct China-aligned clusters converged on a single government target using USB-propagated malware (USBFect/HIUPAN), multiple RATs (FluffyGh0st, Gorem, Masol), and novel loaders (Hypnosis).
incidents.impact: Persistent access established across multiple government endpoints with keylogging, data theft, and backdoor capabilities. The convergence of three independent clusters suggests coordinated, high-priority intelligence collection.

incidents.victim: U.S. Department of Defense and others
incidents.vector: Four IoT botnets (Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid, Mossad) comprising 3 million+ compromised routers and webcams launched hundreds of thousands of DDoS attacks with extortion demands.
incidents.impact: DOJ, FBI, and DCIS seized infrastructure; Aisuru alone issued 200,000+ attack commands. Victims reported tens of thousands of dollars in losses. Botnet operators demanded extortion payments.

incidents.vector: Social engineering campaign where attackers posed as support staff and tricked targets into sharing Signal/WhatsApp security codes, enabling device linking and message interception.
incidents.impact: Thousands of secure messaging accounts compromised, including a former senior German BND intelligence official. FBI, French authorities, and Dutch intelligence all issued warnings.

incidents.vector: Cyberattack targeting internal IT systems of the LA Metro transit system.
incidents.impact: Internal system access restricted. Nature and full scope of the attack remain unclear.

incidents.vector: Breach of employee benefits and retirement funds management platform traced to December 2025.
incidents.impact: 2.7 million Americans had personal data stolen, including Social Security numbers and health plan information.

incidents.victim: Organizations across the Middle East and Gulf states
incidents.vector: Massive surge of conflict-themed phishing, credential harvesting, and financial fraud exploiting the Iran war — 7,381 phishing URLs across 1,881 hostnames, impersonating telecoms, banks, airlines, and government entities.
incidents.impact: Widespread financial fraud, credential theft, and cryptocurrency scams targeting UAE, Saudi Arabia, and broader region. Campaigns impersonate Emirates Post, Dubai Police, Iranian banks, and major telcos.

incidents.vector: Pro-Ukrainian group Bearlyfy deploys custom GenieLocker ransomware on Windows and modified Babuk on Linux, escalating from tools built on leaked LockBit 3 code.
incidents.impact: More than 70 attacks over the past year with ransom demands growing to hundreds of thousands of dollars. Roughly one in five victims pays the ransom.

incidents.vector: International law enforcement operation (Operation Magnus) leading to extradition of alleged RedLine developer Hambardzum Minasyan from Armenia to the U.S.
incidents.impact: Key developer charged with conspiracy to commit access device fraud, CFAA violations, and money laundering. RedLine is one of the world's most prevalent infostealers, responsible for billions of stolen credentials annually.

incidents.victim: Mario Kart/TA-551 and Yanluowang botnet operations
incidents.vector: Russian national Ilya Angelov operated a botnet distributing malware via phishing campaigns reaching 700,000 emails/day, selling compromised machine access to ransomware operators.
incidents.impact: Angelov sentenced to 24 months in U.S. prison; the botnet enabled BitPaymer ransomware attacks generating $14 million+ in ransoms from 72 U.S. networks. Separately, Yanluowang member Aleksei Volkov received 81 months.

incidents.vector: Three individuals conspired to smuggle export-controlled NVIDIA AI chips worth $170 million+ to China through Thai shell companies, signing false end-user certifications.
incidents.impact: DOJ charged a Chinese national and two U.S. citizens; the principal defendant arrested March 22. The scheme sought 600 servers containing export-controlled chips. Text messages showed defendants knowingly evading China embargo.

incidents.vector: Cyber breach affecting internal systems at the Netherlands' Ministry of Finance.
incidents.impact: Details remain limited as an investigation is underway. The ministry is probing the extent of the compromise.

incidents.vector: Cyberattack targeting Puerto Rico's driver's license services agency.
incidents.impact: All driver's license appointments canceled territory-wide while systems are restored.
